articles.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 27 of attacks?
articles.summary: The US and Israel’s war on Iran is intensifying, as Trump again claims Iranian leaders want to 'make a deal'.

articles.title: In U-turn, UK police say Palestine Action protesters will be arrested again
articles.summary: London's Met Police had eased arrests following the High Court ruling that the ban on Palestine Action was unlawful.

articles.title: Deaths and debts: Missiles in Gulf shake millions of South Asian families
articles.summary: Most people killed in Iran's Gulf attacks have been from South Asia. And entire economies back home are on edge.

articles.title: Senegal appeal to CAS against handing over of AFCON title to Morocco
articles.summary: Senegal hope to overturn CAF's decision to crown Morocco champions after a walkout by Senegalese players in the final.

articles.title: NBA owners vote to explore Seattle, Las Vegas expansion bids
articles.summary: All 30 owners voted to explore the two city bids, according to ESPN, with the 2028-29 season the entry point.

articles.title: Netanyahu boasts about ‘breaking boundaries in every sense of the word’
articles.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says the country is working to expand its 'buffer zone' from Lebanon.

articles.title: Oil prices rise higher as Iran denies US talks, dimming deescalation hopes
articles.summary: Brent crude tops $104 a barrel as hopes fade for deescalation in US-Israel war on Iran.

articles.title: Malaysia’s prime minister says Iran talks should end war
articles.summary: Malaysia’s Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim said any talks with Iran must aim to end the war.

articles.title: Videos show wave of missiles fired from Lebanon to Israel
articles.summary: Videos show scenes of rocket interceptions in the sky over central Israel.

articles.title: US lawmakers push for pause in data centres until AI safeguards in place
articles.summary: Bernie Sanders and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez introduce bill to pause AI rollout amid growing backlash to technology.

articles.title: Russia slams second US-Israeli strike at Iran’s Bushehr nuclear reactor
articles.summary: Russia’s foreign ministry has accused the US and Israel of trying to spark a nuclear disaster after a strike at Bushehr.

articles.title: Australia bans visitors from Iran amid war in the Middle East
articles.summary: Australia's Home Affairs Department said decision to ban Iranian visitors amid the war was in the 'national interest'.

articles.title: US jury finds Meta, Google, liable in social media addiction trial
articles.summary: A Los Angeles jury has found Alphabet's Google and Meta liable for $6 million in a landmark social media addiction case.

articles.title: Malaysia moves to tighten rules for expats, raising fears of talent flight
articles.summary: Malaysia's government plans to slash the number of foreign workers to encourage local hiring and raise incomes.

articles.title: Harrison: ‘There is going to be some kind of a military escalation.’
articles.summary: Ross Harrison says public talk of diplomacy between the US and Iran is hiding hardline positions.

articles.title: Tehran issues warning to regional neighbour if Iranian island occupied
articles.summary: Iran's parliament speaker issues threat as US administration talks peace while committing more troops to conflict.

articles.title: Trump says Iranian leaders want deal ‘so badly’ but fear retaliation
articles.summary: Donald Trump said Iran is negotiating with the US but is “afraid to say it”.

articles.title: Sarah Mullally enthroned as first female archbishop of Canterbury
articles.summary: The first woman to lead the Church of England formally installed at ceremony before 2,000 guests.

articles.title: Iran war live: US demands Tehran accept defeat; Israel pounds Lebanon
articles.summary: Iranian official describes US's 15-point plan as 'maximalist' as state television outlines five conditions for peace.

articles.title: Migrants march in southern Mexico to denounce immigration restrictions
articles.summary: The march comes as a court in the US suggests there may be a secret deportation agreement between the US and Mexico.

articles.title: Egypt says it is ready to host Iran de-escalation talks
articles.summary: Egypt’s Foreign Minister Badr Abdelatty said Cairo is ready to host talks on Iran to support de-escalation.

articles.title: Iranian foreign minister rejects talks with US
articles.summary: “No negotiations have taken place.” Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi says Tehran is not in talks with the US.

articles.title: Mexico will continue accepting Cuban medical workers despite US pressure
articles.summary: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum praises services from Cuban doctors, who often work in underserved rural areas.

articles.title: Video shows Israeli strike hitting displacement camp in Gaza
articles.summary: Videos show the moment a strike hit a tent camp for displaced Palestinians in Deir el-Balah.

articles.title: Palestine weekly wrap: West Bank attacks surge, Israel restricts Gaza aid
articles.summary: Settler attacks, aid curbs and land seizures mar a week that was meant to be one of celebration for Palestinians.
